title: Elytrigia parishii
intro: Elytrigia parishii, also known as Elymus parishii, is a perennial grass native to the western United States, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Elytrigia parishii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in salves and poultices to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Elytrigia parishii is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a species of wild ryegrass, and its full classification is Elytrigia parishii (S.Wats.) Á.Löve. The genus Elytrigia is a part of the tribe Triticeae. The species is native to the western United States. Its classification is also sometimes expressed in the synonym Elymus parishii.
